value.title: Global VC Investment Surged In January, With U.S. Dominating Funding But A Pair Of AI Model IPOs In China
value.content: Global venture funding posted strong gains in January, with $55 billion invested in startups around the world, Crunchbase data shows, with funding  more than doubling from $25.5 billion a year earlier and up over 50% from December.

value.title: Soaring Veradermics IPO Shows Investor Interest In Hair-Loss Companies Isn’t Thinning
value.content: Veradermics, a developer of an oral treatment for pattern hair loss, began trading on the New York Stock Exchange with shares more than doubling in early first-day trading.

value.title: Google Is A $4T Company That’s Still In The Startup Game
value.content: Google last year partook in the largest number of startup deals in recent years, Crunchbase data shows, and those rounds had the highest collective value since the market peak four years ago.

value.title: Y Combinator Was By Far The Most-Active Fintech Investor in 2025, Data Shows
value.content: All told, global venture funding to financial technology startups totaled $51.8 billion for 2025 compared to $40.8 billion in 2024, per Crunchbase data. The investors who provided all that capital included a mix of private equity and alternative investors, with venture capital firms and accelerators next in line.

value.title: Q&A: Why Deep Tech Investors Are Turning To The Secondary Market For Liquidity
value.content: Crunchbase News sat down with Sriram Viswanathan, founding managing partner of Celesta Capital, to discuss why the secondary market is accelerating now, how it is impacting deep tech companies, and where opportunity lies in deep tech in general.

value.title: The Week’s 10 Biggest Funding Rounds: AI Megadeals Dominate Again
value.content: This week’s largest fundraising recipient was frontier AI lab Recursive Intelligence, and agentic AI, AI-compute, and AI-native startups filled up much of the rest of the Top 10 list.

value.title: 6 Trends In Tech And Startups We’re Watching In 2026, From An IPO Boom To More Huge AI Deals
value.content: Last year was the third-strongest on record for global venture funding with a surprisingly strong year for IPOs and an uptick in startup M&A numbers, and industry insiders we spoke with expect 2026 will be another robust year. With that, we provide a closer look at six trends we expect to see unfold this year.
